title: HR Generalist
description:
Job Summary

We are seeking a highly driven and experienced HR Generalist to support a fast-growing beauty and salon group with multiple outlets across Singapore. The ideal candidate will be hands-on, people-oriented, and operationally strong—capable of managing end-to-end HR functions in a dynamic, multi-branch environment.

Responsibilities

  • Lead full-cycle recruitment by sourcing, screening, interviewing, and selecting candidates to fulfill staffing needs for salon and headquarters roles
  • Execute employee onboarding and offboarding processes, including preparing contracts, managing documentation, and delivering orientation programs to ensure smooth transitions
  • Administer payroll processing, attendance tracking, overtime calculation, leave management, and employee benefits to maintain accurate compensation and compliance
  • Ensure adherence to MOM regulations, Singapore Employment Act, and company HR policies by monitoring and updating HR practices accordingly
  • Manage work pass applications and renewals, coordinating related HR documentation to support workforce legality
  • Serve as the primary contact for employee relations by addressing grievances and disciplinary matters promptly and fairly
  • Maintain accurate HR records, personnel files, and HR information systems to support data integrity and reporting needs
  • Coordinate performance review cycles, probation confirmations, and contract renewals to support talent management and retention
  • Support training programs, staff engagement initiatives, and internal HR projects to foster employee development and workplace culture
  • Collaborate with salon managers to assess manpower planning and staffing requirements, ensuring operational efficiency across multiple branches
  • Prepare and deliver HR reports, headcount tracking, and recruitment metrics to provide management with actionable insights

Required competencies and certifications

  • Solid understanding of Singapore Employment Act and MOM guidelines to ensure legal compliance in HR operations

Preferred competencies and qualifications

  • Diploma or Bachelor’s Degree in Human Resources, Business Administration, or related field
  • Minimum 2–5 years of HR Generalist experience, preferably within beauty, retail, F&B, hospitality, or multi-outlet operations
  • Proven ability to manage high-volume recruitment effectively
  • Proficiency in MS Office and HR systems to streamline HR administrative tasks
  • Strong communication, interpersonal, and stakeholder management skills demonstrated through effective collaboration and conflict resolution
  • Highly organized and detail-oriented with the ability to multitask and prioritize responsibilities across multiple locations
  • Capacity to work independently in a fast-paced environment, demonstrating initiative and problem-solving skills
